req.title: Hr-Admin updates payroll-run
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to update a payroll-run through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers four distinct test dimensions: (1) a valid hr-admin updating a payroll-run end-to-end with audit verification (EP positive), (2) submission with a missing mandatory field producing a 400 validation error (NEG/DT negative), (3) a boundary value on a numeric field at its maximum allowed length/value (BVA boundary), (4) an unauthenticated request receiving 401 (NEG negative), and (5) a forbidden-role request receiving 403 (DT negative). State transition from 'draft' to 'updated' is also exercised in the positive case (STC). Tests use Playwright for UI interaction where appropriate and a thin API helper for audit verification. All tests are self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up their own payroll-run records.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_updates_payroll_run_successfully
preconditions: A payroll-run record exists in state 'draft'
actions: Fill all mandatory fields with valid data
Verify success response (200 or 201) with entity id
Query audit log for the update event
expected: Audit record written with actor=hr-admin and action=UPDATE
trace: R-WEB-038: positive path - valid update by hr-admin
R-WEB-038: audit record written on success
R-WEB-038: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_hr_admin_updates_payroll_run_successfully(
    hr_admin_page,        # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright page for hr-admin
    create_payroll_run,   # <fixture>: creates a draft payroll-run, yields {"id": str, ...}
    audit_api_client,     # <fixture>: thin REST client for audit-log queries
    valid_payroll_payload # <fixture>: dict of valid mandatory field values
):
    run = create_payroll_run(state="draft")
    run_id = run["id"]
    page = hr_admin_page

    page.goto(f"/payroll-runs/{run_id}/edit")
    <helper_fn>.fill_payroll_form(page, valid_payroll_payload)
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='success-banner']")).to_be_visible()
    entity_id_text = page.locator("[data-testid='entity-id']").inner_text()
    assert entity_id_text, "Expected entity id in success banner"

    audit = audit_api_client.get_latest_event(entity_id=run_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert audit["actor_role"] == "hr-admin"
    assert audit["action"] == "UPDATE"
    assert audit["entity_id"] == run_id

tests.name: test_update_payroll_run_missing_mandatory_field_returns_validation_error
expected: Form does not navigate away (client-side or server-side 400 response)
Validation error message is displayed for the empty mandatory field
trace: R-WEB-038: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_update_payroll_run_missing_mandatory_field_returns_validation_error(
    hr_admin_page,
    create_payroll_run,
    audit_api_client,
    valid_payroll_payload
):
    run = create_payroll_run(state="draft")
    run_id = run["id"]
    page = hr_admin_page

    page.goto(f"/payroll-runs/{run_id}/edit")
    payload_missing_field = {k: v for k, v in valid_payroll_payload.items()
                             if k != "pay_period_end"}
    <helper_fn>.fill_payroll_form(page, payload_missing_field)
    page.locator("[name='pay_period_end']").fill("")  # ensure field is blank
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    error_locator = page.locator("[data-testid='field-error-pay_period_end']")
    expect(error_locator).to_be_visible()
    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='success-banner']")).not_to_be_visible()

    events = audit_api_client.get_events(entity_id=run_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert len(events) == 0, "No audit record should be written on validation failure"

tests.name: test_update_payroll_run_description_at_max_length_boundary
preconditions: System enforces a maximum length of 255 chars on the 'description' field
actions: Fill mandatory fields with valid data
Repeat with 256 characters and verify rejection
expected: 256-char description: validation error shown, no audit record written
trace: R-WEB-038: boundary value on description field max length
R-WEB-038: response 200 or 201 with entity id on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

MAX_LEN = 255

def test_update_payroll_run_description_at_max_length_boundary(
    hr_admin_page,
    create_payroll_run,
    audit_api_client,
    valid_payroll_payload
):
    page = hr_admin_page

    # --- BVA: exactly at max (should succeed) ---
    run_ok = create_payroll_run(state="draft")
    page.goto(f"/payroll-runs/{run_ok['id']}/edit")
    payload_ok = {**valid_payroll_payload, "description": "A" * MAX_LEN}
    <helper_fn>.fill_payroll_form(page, payload_ok)
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")
    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='success-banner']")).to_be_visible()
    audit_ok = audit_api_client.get_latest_event(entity_id=run_ok["id"], action="UPDATE")
    assert audit_ok["entity_id"] == run_ok["id"]

    # --- BVA: one over max (should fail) ---
    run_fail = create_payroll_run(state="draft")
    page.goto(f"/payroll-runs/{run_fail['id']}/edit")
    payload_over = {**valid_payroll_payload, "description": "A" * (MAX_LEN + 1)}
    <helper_fn>.fill_payroll_form(page, payload_over)
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")
    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='field-error-description']")).to_be_visible()
    events = audit_api_client.get_events(entity_id=run_fail["id"], action="UPDATE")
    assert len(events) == 0

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_payroll_run
preconditions: No active session / user is unauthenticated
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_payroll_run(
    api_client,          # <fixture>: unauthenticated requests.Session-like client
    create_payroll_run,  # <fixture>: creates a draft payroll-run via privileged API
    audit_api_client,
    valid_payroll_payload
):
    run = create_payroll_run(state="draft")
    run_id = run["id"]

    response = api_client.put(
        f"/payroll-runs/{run_id}",
        json=valid_payroll_payload,
        headers={}  # explicitly no Authorization header
    )

    assert response.status_code == 401, (
        f"Expected 401, got {response.status_code}"
    )
    events = audit_api_client.get_events(entity_id=run_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert len(events) == 0, "No audit record should exist for unauthenticated attempt"

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_update_payroll_run
preconditions: User is authenticated with role 'employee' (not hr-admin)
actions: Send PUT /payroll-runs/{id} with employee role auth token
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_forbidden_role_cannot_update_payroll_run(
    employee_api_client,  # <fixture>: authenticated client for role=employee
    create_payroll_run,
    audit_api_client,
    valid_payroll_payload
):
    run = create_payroll_run(state="draft")
    run_id = run["id"]

    response = employee_api_client.put(
        f"/payroll-runs/{run_id}",
        json=valid_payroll_payload
    )

    assert response.status_code == 403, (
        f"Expected 403 Forbidden, got {response.status_code}"
    )
    events = audit_api_client.get_events(entity_id=run_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert len(events) == 0, "No audit record should exist for forbidden role attempt"

tests.name: test_payroll_run_state_transitions_draft_to_updated
actions: Attempt a second identical update (idempotency check)
expected: After second submit: state still == 'updated', no duplicate audit records
trace: R-WEB-038: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_payroll_run_state_transitions_draft_to_updated(
    hr_admin_page,
    create_payroll_run,
    payroll_run_api_client,  # <fixture>: privileged read-only API client
    audit_api_client,
    valid_payroll_payload
):
    run = create_payroll_run(state="draft")
    run_id = run["id"]
    page = hr_admin_page

    # First update: draft -> updated
    page.goto(f"/payroll-runs/{run_id}/edit")
    <helper_fn>.fill_payroll_form(page, valid_payroll_payload)
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")
    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='success-banner']")).to_be_visible()

    record = payroll_run_api_client.get(f"/payroll-runs/{run_id}").json()
    assert record["state"] == "updated"

    # Second update (idempotency)
    page.goto(f"/payroll-runs/{run_id}/edit")
    <helper_fn>.fill_payroll_form(page, valid_payroll_payload)
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")
    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='success-banner']")).to_be_visible()

    record2 = payroll_run_api_client.get(f"/payroll-runs/{run_id}").json()
    assert record2["state"] == "updated"

    audit_events = audit_api_client.get_events(entity_id=run_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert len(audit_events) >= 1
